heading: Retirement for special trial judges
label: 7447A Retirement for special trial judges
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–328, div. T, title VII, § 702(d)136 Stat. 5404Dec. 29, 2022“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 3121 and 7448 of this title and , The Public Health and Welfare] shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [], except that section 7447A(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by this section) shall take effect on the date that is 180 days after such date of enactment. Special trial judges retiring on or after the date of the enactment of this Act, and before the date that is 180 days after the date of such enactment, may file an election under such section not later than 60 days after such date.”section 410 of Title 42Dec. 29, 2022

children.heading: Retirement
children.label: 1 Retirement
children.text: Any special trial judge appointed pursuant to section 7443A may retire from service as a special trial judge if the individual meets the age and service requirements set forth in the following table:If the special trial judge has attained age:And the years of service as a special trial judge are at least:651566146713681269117010.

children.heading: Length of service
children.label: 2 Length of service
children.text: In making any determination of length of service as a special trial judge there shall be included all periods (whether or not consecutive) during which an individual served as a special trial judge

children.heading: Retirement upon disability
children.label: b Retirement upon disability
children.text: Any special trial judge appointed pursuant to section 7443A who becomes permanently disabled from performing such individual’s duties shall retire from service as a special trial judge.

children.heading: Recalling of retired special trial judges
children.label: c Recalling of retired special trial judges

children.text: the aggregate of such periods in any 1 calendar year shall not (without the consent of such individual) exceed 90 calendar days, and
such individual shall be relieved of performing such duties during any period in which illness or disability precludes the performance of such duties.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of years such individual has served as special trial judge bears to,

children.label: A
children.text: at a rate equal to the rate of the salary payable to a special trial judge during such period, if the individual had at least 10 years of service as a special trial judge before retirement, and

children.label: B
children.text: at a rate equal to ½ the rate described in subparagraph (A), if the individual had fewer than 10 years of service as a special trial judge before retirement.

children.heading: Beginning date and payment
children.label: 3 Beginning date and payment
children.text: Retired pay under this subsection shall begin to accrue on the day following the date on which the individual’s salary as a special trial judge ceases to accrue, and shall continue to accrue during the remainder of such individual’s life. Retired pay under this subsection shall be paid in the same manner as the salary of a special trial judge.

children.heading: Partial years
children.label: 4 Partial years
children.text: In computing the rate of the retired pay for an individual to whom paragraph (1) applies, any portion of the aggregate number of years such individual has served as a special trial judge which is a fractional part of 1 year shall be eliminated if it is less than 6 months, or shall be counted as a full year if it is 6 months or more.

children.heading: Recalled service
children.label: 5 Recalled service
children.text: In computing the rate of the retired pay for an individual to whom paragraph (1) applies, any period during which such individual performs services under subsection (c) on a substantially full-time basis shall be treated as a period during which such individual has served as a special trial judge.

children.heading: Election to Receive Retired Pay
children.label: e Election to Receive Retired Pay

children.text: may be made only while an individual is a special trial judge (except that in the case of an individual who fails to be reappointed as a special trial judge, such election may be made within 60 days after such individual leaves office as a special trial judge),
once made, shall be irrevocable, and
shall be made by filing notice thereof in writing with the chief judge.

children.heading: Other rules made applicable
children.label: f Other rules made applicable
children.text: The rules of subsections (f), (g), (h)(2), (i), and (j), and the first sentence of subsection (h)(1), of section 7447 shall apply to a special trial judge in the same manner as a judge of the Tax Court. For purposes of the preceding sentence, any reference to the President in such subsections shall be applied as if it were a reference to the chief judge.
